main: ^Oh, my dear, you look so scared. What bothers your young soul?
^Ah… I’m… I think I’m seeing things in the fog, lola. They move and they whisper things I don’t understand…
^Oh, my love. Come, hug me. War is so cruel to young souls such as you, caught in its crossfire…
^At this shrine? Isn’t this a Buddhist shrine?
^Haha, yes, you are sharp, my love.

^It’s alright. You do not have to pray. I believe the serenity of a small shrine surviving the fires of war is enough to bring solace, isn’t it? Our own churches have been razed and burned down, but this… such a tiny thing can be resilient. Like you.
^Do you feel at peace, my dear?
^But what about you? What happened to your family…? Where is Kuya Lee?
^Ah, my son… (her smile drops a little bit) He was on his way home from Mintal last night. As for me… I’m unhurt.
^Would you like to come with me, lola? We can look for him together.
^Oh, my dear, do not worry yourself over me. I will wait for him here, just as I had promised. Run along now. I know you have places to be.
^Those things you hear in your ear and the things you see in the fog… if they call for you, do not go near them, alright?
^Now go. I wish you luck on your journey. If you see my little Lee on the way here, tell him to come home soon.
